2603.16856.title: Online Experiential Learning for Language Models
2603.16856.paper_summary: The prevailing paradigm for improving large language models relies on offline training with human annotations or simulated environments, leaving the rich experience accumulated during real-world deployment entirely unexploited. We propose Online Experiential Learning (OEL), a framework that enables language models to continuously improve from their own deployment experience. OEL operates in two stages: first, transferable experiential knowledge is extracted and accumulated from interaction trajectories collected on the user side; second, this knowledge is consolidated into model parameters via on-policy context distillation, requiring no access to the user-side environment. The two stages are iterated to form an online learning loop, where the improved model collects higher-quality trajectories that yield richer experiential knowledge for subsequent rounds. We evaluate OEL on text-based game environments across multiple model scales and both thinking and non-thinking variants. OEL achieves consistent improvements over successive iterations, enhancing both task accuracy and token efficiency while preserving out-of-distribution performance. Our analysis further shows that extracted experiential knowledge is significantly more effective than raw trajectories, and that on-policy consistency between the knowledge source and the policy model is critical for effective learning.
2603.16856.paper_summary_zh: 目前改善大型語言模型的主流範式依賴於使用人類標註或模擬環境的離線訓練，完全未利用在真實世界部署中積累的豐富經驗。我們提出了在線經驗學習（OEL），這是一個使語言模型能夠從自身部署經驗中持續改進的框架。OEL運作分為兩個階段：首先，從用戶端收集的交互軌跡中提取並累積可轉移的經驗知識；其次，通過政策內上下文蒸餾將這些知識整合到模型參數中，無需訪問用戶端環境。這兩個階段反覆迭代，形成一個在線學習循環，改進後的模型收集更高質量的軌跡，從而為後續輪次提供更豐富的經驗知識。我們在多個模型規模及思考和非思考變體的文本遊戲環境中評估了OEL。OEL在連續迭代中實現了一致的改進，提升了任務準確性和標記效率，同時保持了分佈外性能。我們的分析進一步顯示，提取的經驗知識顯著比原始軌跡更有效，並且知識來源與政策模型之間的政策內一致性對於有效學習至關重要。
2603.16856.author: Tianzhu Ye et.al.
2603.16856.authors: Tianzhu Ye, Li Dong, Qingxiu Dong, Xun Wu, Shaohan Huang, Furu Wei
